name: conservation_biology_biometric_millhaven_auto_ben
premise: A Conservation Biology response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving conservation biology biometric millhaven auto ben. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is in the next hour. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as autonomy vs beneficence under pressure. A whistleblower has already contacted media; the narrative is forming now. Constraints: Ensure a usable human override/appeals path. Preserve evidence under audit and litigation-hold.

agents.name: Elena Navarro
agents.identity: I am Elena Navarro, accountable for a defensible call in Conservation Biology when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays beneficence for autonomy.

agents.name: Sana Nielsen
agents.identity: I am Sana Nielsen, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Conservation Biology.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so beneficence isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of autonomy.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect beneficence.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to beneficence to secure autonomy under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is in the next hour. Evidence is partial. Elena Navarro must decide under autonomy vs beneficence. What does Elena Navarro do?
